name: PropelAuth API
what_it_does: B2B authentication and user management platform providing organizations, role-based access, SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, and hosted login UI for multi-tenant SaaS applications.
use_cases: Add multi-tenant authentication with org-level RBAC to agent platforms serving multiple customers
Enable enterprise SSO (SAML/OIDC) for B2B customers without building the integration from scratch
Automate user provisioning and deprovisioning via SCIM for enterprise directory sync
Manage organization invitations, memberships, and role assignments via API
Issue user and org-scoped tokens consumed by downstream agent services
not_for: Consumer-facing authentication (B2C) — PropelAuth is purpose-built for B2B org structures
Simple single-tenant apps that don't need organization management or enterprise SSO
best_when: Best when building multi-tenant agent platforms where enterprise customers need SSO, SCIM, and org-level RBAC — removes months of auth infrastructure work.
avoid_when: Avoid for consumer apps without organizational hierarchy, or when your user base is small enough that a simpler auth provider covers all requirements at lower cost.

auth.notes: Backend integration uses API keys for server-to-server calls. User-facing flows use OIDC/OAuth 2.0. Access tokens are JWTs verifiable with PropelAuth's public keys — no round-trip required for verification.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1,000 monthly active users (MAU) on free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Paid plans from $199/month including SAML SSO and SCIM; enterprise pricing for unlimited MAU
pricing.notes: Free tier covers development and early-stage products. SAML SSO and SCIM are gated behind paid plans — required for enterprise customer requirements.

performance.rate_limits: Backend API rate limits are generous and documented per endpoint; token verification is local (JWT) with no API call needed

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns structured error objects with clear codes; SDK wrappers surface typed exceptions for common failure modes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations and token verification are idempotent. User and org creation/mutation should be treated as non-idempotent unless using explicit external IDs.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT verification requires fetching PropelAuth's public key on startup — cache this to avoid cold-start latency
SAML configuration per org adds operational complexity — agents managing org provisioning must handle SAML metadata exchange
SCIM provisioning events arrive as webhooks — agents relying on real-time directory sync must handle webhook delivery failures
MAU counting can be surprising in agent contexts where service accounts or bots are treated as users

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 certified. JWTs signed with RS256; public key verification avoids credential exposure in verification path. SAML and SCIM implementations follow established enterprise security standards.
